2603.19427.title: Vocabulary shapes cross-lingual variation of word-order learnability in language models
2603.19427.paper_summary: Why do some languages like Czech permit free word order, while others like English do not? We address this question by pretraining transformer language models on a spectrum of synthetic word-order variants of natural languages. We observe that greater word-order irregularity consistently raises model surprisal, indicating reduced learnability. Sentence reversal, however, affects learnability only weakly. A coarse distinction of free- (e.g., Czech and Finnish) and fixed-word-order languages (e.g., English and French) does not explain cross-lingual variation. Instead, the structure of the word and subword vocabulary strongly predicts the model surprisal. Overall, vocabulary structure emerges as a key driver of computational word-order learnability across languages.
2603.19427.paper_summary_zh: 為什麼某些語言如捷克語允許自由的詞序，而其他語言如英語則不允許？我們通過在一系列合成的自然語言詞序變體上預訓練Transformer語言模型來解決這個問題。<lb/>我們觀察到，更大的詞序不規則性持續提高模型的驚訝度，顯示學習能力降低。<lb/>然而，句子反轉對學習能力的影響僅微弱。<lb/>對自由詞序（例如捷克語和芬蘭語）和固定詞序語言（例如英語和法語）進行粗略區分並不能解釋跨語言的變異。<lb/>相反，詞彙和子詞彙的結構強烈預測模型的驚訝度。<lb/>總體而言，詞彙結構成為跨語言計算詞序學習能力的關鍵驅動因素。
2603.19427.author: Jonas Mayer Martins et.al.
2603.19427.authors: Jonas Mayer Martins, Jaap Jumelet, Viola Priesemann, Lisa Beinborn
